The word will direct and quicken prayer, for by it the Spirit helps our weaknesses in prayer. Careful study of God's word will increasingly reveal to us our own sinfulness and the abundance of his salvation; thus it calls us to mourn for sin and to rejoice in him. Every discovery of the truth of God should make us more diligent in attending to his sacred word and his worship.

We have here a summary of their prayers recorded. Much more, no doubt, was said. Whatever ability we have to do anything in the way of duty, we are to serve and glorify God according to the full extent of it. When confessing our sins, it is good to notice the mercies of God, so that we may be more humbled and ashamed. The Lord’s dealings showed his goodness and patience, and the hardness of their hearts. The testimony of the prophets was the testimony of the Spirit in the prophets, and it was the Spirit of Christ in them. They spoke as they were moved by the Holy Ghost, and what they said is to be received accordingly. The result was wonder at the Lord’s mercies and the feeling that sin had brought them to their present state, from which nothing but unmerited love could rescue them. And is not their conduct a reflection of human nature? Let us study the history of our land and our own history. Let us remember our advantages from childhood and ask what our first responses were. Let us frequently do so, that we may be kept humble, thankful, and watchful. Let all remember that pride and stubbornness are sins which ruin the soul. But it is often as hard to persuade those who are heartbroken to hope as formerly it was to bring them to fear. Is this your case? Behold this sweet promise: A God ready to pardon! Instead of staying away from God under a sense of unworthiness, let us come boldly to the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy and find grace to help in time of need. He is a God ready to pardon.
